scenario.context: Station 06 of the day run: implement JSON files, web services, and database functionality.
scenario.mission: You stabilize data exchange between components and secure error handling as well as consistency.

customerRequest.subject: Station 06: Integration logic
customerRequest.body: Please implement JSON import/export, web-service calls, and database integration in a robust way.

questions.title: Secure JSON import
questions.prompt: What is most important before processing incoming JSON data?

feedback.correct: Correct: Validation is the required first step.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without structured validation, processing remains unstable.

questions.title: Use web service robustly
questions.prompt: Which points improve API usage? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Define timeouts and a retry strategy
Evaluate error codes and handle them logically
Test version changes in a controlled way

feedback.correct: Correct: This makes API usage reliable.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure failure and version management more strongly.

questions.title: Order integration flow
questions.prompt: Put the flow for a consistent data integration in order.

feedback.correct: Correct: The flow is linked cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Validation and plausibility checks have to happen before the DB step.

questions.title: Import volume
questions.prompt: Each run imports 85 records. With 6 runs, how many records in total?

feedback.wrong: Please multiply the amount per run by the number of runs.

questions.title: Document integration logic
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences the secure flow from JSON through the API to the database.
questions.expectedOutline: Step chain with error handling and consistency reference.

feedback.correct: Correct: The integration logic is documented in a practical way.
feedback.wrong: Please describe validation, the API step, and DB consistency more concretely.
